notes: Didn't need to disable esync, game launched with no tweaks. Performance is fantastic - enabling vsync gives the performance a major hit. title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes:
The first time I launched the game, I alt-tabbed around in between the splash screen loading and the game loading, and it crashed. Believing that tweaks were required, I opted for Wine D3D over DXVK, and the game launched successfully. However, I noticed significant artifacts, so I also disabled ESYNC, which resolved that issue. But performance was poor.

Upon subsequent runs, I tried removing those tweaks and lo and behold: the game still ran! Not only that, but it ran well, becoming playable in 4K with all settings maxed. There is still stuttering in new areas, which I understand to be a defect of the PC port.

I do have a large open file limit on my machine, which may be the reason that ESYNC is fine for me when DXVK is used (aka, no tweaks at all). If you don't know what that is, look into raising the hard limit of type nofile in your ulimit configuration with a web search for those terms.

I recommend the -nointro launch argument. The introductory series of logos and copyright notices is very long.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Game crashes when Esync is disabled, and doesn't make it past the main menu when VSync is enabled. Also I enabled -nointro because the intro is obnoxiously and unskippably long, Apart from that, works perfectly, even with Steam Controller.

notes: Bioshock Remastered will start and run without any alteration to the Launcher. But, the textures will not load, and where they are supposed to be will look like they are slathered in Vaseline. The game will also load and save very slowly. But when you add the following arguments to the Launcher (do not include the quotation marks): " PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% " it runs perfectly. All of the textures, shadowing and lighting are perfect, and the game loads and runs perfectly.

notes: On Proton it doesn't work out of the box, you need to set "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro" to get it working perfectly fine with DXVK (D3D11 to Vulkan translation). Additional mouse input didn't work until I disabled the controller in the game options. If esync is enabled, the game will freeze during loading a new game. Tested with RADV and the new Mesa ACO compiler on Plasma Wayland.

notes:
Disable Esync in launch options with: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro
This also disables the incredibly long and tedious introduction whenever the game starts. There is still one noticeable visual glitch that occurs though. When you get to the rare scenes where you get flashing lights they appear as alternating horizontal bars of black and whatever you're supposed to see instead. There's only two locations early in the game where I've noticed this though, from there on it seems just about perfect.

notes: At first the game would attempt to launch in fullscreen and wouldn't display anything. Took me all of three seconds to figure out it was DWM (my window manager) at fault and I simply launched the game with the -windowed option. Runs perfectly out of the box on Gentoo.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Without "use WineD3d11" the game crashed several times per hour (thanks to whoever recommended that); restarting it usually gave a dialog asking to reset to default settings, and about half the time the game would immediately crash if I selected "no", even though the only change was brightness. With it enabled, it stops crashing. It does perform a little worse now, but it's not bad enough for me to rate silver, and it doesn't crash. Disabling Esync made textures load faster and saving or starting a hack no longer takes over a minute.

notes: The game runs pretty well, but not without issues. It doesn't like alt-tabbing, and sometimes likes to crash sometimes when saving the game, and has a horrible stutter when loading new areas within the level. To avoid alt-tab issues, I run the game in Windowed mode, but make it fullscreen with my window manager. To minimise repeating large chunks of gameplay due to save crashing, I save the game regularly. Launching the game with "-ReadTexturePoolFromIni" seems to reduce the severity of the stuttering when loading new areas. Overall not a great experience, even if the performance is fine.
